---
title: "Regulatory Agency Budget Outlays by Sector"
author: "Henry Hirsch"
date: "2026-03-20"
output: html_document
---

## Initialize

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(here)
i_am("charts/code/sector_reg_budget_outlays.Rmd")

source(here("charts", "code", "local_utils.R"))
source(here("charts", "code", "style.R"))

data_file <- "sector_regulatory_agency_budget_outlays_by_fy.csv"
copy_dataset(data_file, here("data"), here("charts", "data"))
```

## Load Data

```{r load_data}
# load csv using here and remove notes from bottom
fig_sector <- read.csv(here("charts", "data", data_file)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(suppressWarnings(as.integer(year))))
```

## Process Data

```{r process}
fig_sector <- fig_sector %>% select(-any_of("X"))

fig_sector <- fig_sector %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(.data$year),
    across(where(is.character), readr::parse_number),
    across(where(is.numeric) & !matches("^year$"), ~ tidyr::replace_na(.x, 0))
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !matches("^year$"), ~ .x / 1000)) %>%
  rename(industry_specific_regulation = `industry.specific_regulation`)

current_date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y")
caption_text <- paste("Source: FY 2024 Regulators' Budget report", "\n\nUpdated:", current_date)
```

## Generate Sector Charts

```{r sector_charts}
economic_sectors <- c("finance_and_banking", "industry_specific_regulation", "general_business")

all_sectors <- c(
  "consumer_safety_and_health",
  "homeland_security",
  "transportation",
  "workplace",
  "environment_and_energy",
  "finance_and_banking",
  "industry_specific_regulation",
  "general_business"
)

sector_labels <- c(
  consumer_safety_and_health   = "Consumer Safety and Health",
  homeland_security            = "Homeland Security",
  transportation               = "Transportation",
  workplace                    = "Workplace",
  environment_and_energy       = "Environment and Energy",
  finance_and_banking          = "Finance and Banking",
  industry_specific_regulation = "Industry-Specific Regulation",
  general_business             = "General Business"
)

color_economic <- "#0d9bd8"
color_social   <- "#0d4a6d"

out <- here("charts", "output", "by_sector")
if (!dir.exists(out)) dir.create(out, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (sector in all_sectors) {

  is_economic  <- sector %in% economic_sectors
  fill_color   <- if (is_economic) color_economic else color_social
  reg_type     <- if (is_economic) "Economic Regulation" else "Social Regulation"
  sector_label <- sector_labels[[sector]]

  df_sector <- fig_sector %>%
    select(year, value = all_of(sector))

  max_val  <- max(df_sector$value, na.rm = TRUE)
  interval <- dplyr::case_when(
    max_val < 2  ~ 0.5,
    max_val < 3  ~ 0.5,
    max_val < 5  ~ 1,
    max_val < 10 ~ 2,
    max_val < 20 ~ 5,
    TRUE         ~ 10
  )
  y_upper_s <- ceiling(max_val / interval) * interval

  p <- ggplot(df_sector, aes(x = year, y = value)) +
    geom_area(fill = fill_color, color = NA) +
    annotate(
      "segment",
      x    = min(df_sector$year, na.rm = TRUE),
      xend = max(df_sector$year, na.rm = TRUE),
      y = 0, yend = 0,
      linewidth = 1,
      color = RSCgray
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_RSC +
    theme(
      plot.title            = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 25)),
      axis.title.x          = element_text(size = 10, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 3)),
      axis.text.x           = element_text(angle = 65, hjust = 1),
      axis.ticks.x          = element_blank(),
      plot.caption          = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15)),
      plot.caption.position = "plot"
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste0("Regulatory Agency Budget Outlays: ", sector_label)) +
    ylab("Billions of Constant 2012 U.S. Dollars") +
    xlab(if (sector == "homeland_security") {
      "\nNote: The Homeland Security sector is a component of the Social Regulation category. TSA, which is displayed separately in the main outlays chart, is included here."
    } else {
      paste0("\nNote: The ", sector_label, " sector is a component of the ", reg_type, " category.")
    }) +
    labs(caption = caption_text) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = seq(0, y_upper_s, by = interval),
      expand = c(0, 0),
      limits = c(0, y_upper_s)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = seq(min(df_sector$year, na.rm = TRUE), max(df_sector$year, na.rm = TRUE), by = 2),
      limits = c(min(df_sector$year, na.rm = TRUE), max(df_sector$year, na.rm = TRUE)),
      expand = c(0, 0)
    )

  p_logo <- suppressWarnings({
    ggdraw() +
      draw_plot(p) +
      draw_image(logo, x = 0.095, y = 0.040, halign = 0, valign = 0, width = 0.2)
  })

  file_stem <- paste0(sector, "_regulatory_outlays_by_fy")

ggsave(
  here("charts", "output", "by_sector", paste0(file_stem, ".pdf")),
  plot = p_logo, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300
)
ggsave(
  here("charts", "output", "by_sector", paste0(file_stem, ".png")),
  device = "png", plot = p_logo,
  width = 1200, height = 900, units = "px", dpi = 96, bg = "white"
)

  message("Saved: ", file_stem)
}
```
